option_B: Insertion of a metal plate to fix the fracture
option_C: Insertion of an intercostal drain to avoid pneumothorax
option_D: Immediate surgical intervention to remove damaged ribs
gemini_raw:
```json
{
  "choice": "C",
  "abstain_code": null,
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "rationale": "The patient has a hemothorax, which is an accumulation of blood in the pleural space. The standard treatment for a hemothorax, even a small one, is the insertion of an intercostal drain (chest tube) to evacuate the blood, prevent a clotted hemothorax, and monitor for ongoing bleeding. This also helps prevent a tension pneumothorax and allows the lung to re-expand."
}
```
